meta.description: Crosswalk between Geolytics Fracture Ontology classes and GSO/Loop3D Geoscience Ontology structural geology classes. Follows the same SKOS-based mapping pattern as data/osdu-gso-crosswalk.json.
sources: Brodaric B., Richard S. — Geoscience Ontology (GSO) v1.0.2. GSC Open File 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296, 2021
Fjaer E. et al. — Petroleum Related Rock Mechanics, 2nd ed. Elsevier 2008
Fossen H. — Structural Geology, 2nd ed. Cambridge University Press 2016
meta.license_note: Use respects CC BY 4.0 of GSO; cite Brodaric & Richard 2021, GSC OF 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296.

match_kinds.closeMatch: Similar but with scope or definition nuances — use with caution in cross-queries.
match_kinds.broadMatch: Geolytics fracture class is more specific than GSO class.
match_kinds.narrowMatch: Geolytics fracture class is more general than GSO class.

mappings.note: Both define Fault as a discrete surface or zone with shear displacement separating two rock masses. The Geolytics definition adds geomechanical context (Anderson regimes, stress controls) not present in GSO.

Both represent the broader zone of deformation surrounding a fault slip surface. Geolytics adds the Caine et al. (1996) three-component architecture (core, damage zone, host rock) not explicitly in GSO.
Kinematic definition is identical: hanging wall descends relative to footwall in extensional setting. Geolytics adds Anderson stress regime condition (Sv > SHmax > Shmin).

Kinematic definition identical: hanging wall ascends in compressional setting. Geolytics distinguishes thrust (< 30 deg) from reverse fault (> 30 deg) as sub-types, adds Anderson condition (SHmax > Shmin > Sv).
Both define lateral (strike-parallel) dominant displacement. Geolytics adds Anderson condition (SHmax > Sv > Shmin) and sub-types (sinistral, dextral) implied but not enumerated here.
GSO Fracture is the generic class for any brittle discontinuity; Geolytics Joint is specifically an opening-mode (Mode I) fracture. Joint is a sub-type of Fracture.

GSO Deformation is broader (any change in shape or position); Geolytics DeformationMechanism focuses on the physical process (brittle fracturing, plastic flow, pressure solution). Use closeMatch — do not substitute 1:1.
GSO JointSet is restricted to joints (Mode I); Geolytics FractureSet encompasses any sub-parallel group of fractures regardless of mode (joints, shear fractures). Broader scope in Geolytics.
